_note: Default profile -- mirrors the hardcoded values in windows.py. Use as a reference or starting point.

_compliance_notes._section_identity: --- DEVICE IDENTITY (sent in SOAP enrollment + SyncML) ---
_compliance_notes.os_version: Reported as SwV in SyncML and OSVersion in SOAP. Intune min/max OS version compliance checks use this. Match or exceed the policy threshold.
_compliance_notes.os_platform: String shown in Intune portal (e.g. 'Windows 10 Enterprise', 'Windows 11 Enterprise'). Informational only.
_compliance_notes.os_edition_enroll: OSEdition sent in SOAP enrollment context. 72 = Enterprise. Does not affect compliance directly -- use os_edition_syncml for that.
_compliance_notes.os_edition_syncml: Reported via DeviceStatus/OS/Edition and WindowsLicensing/Edition CSPs. 4 = Enterprise, 48 = Professional. Compliance policies requiring Enterprise edition check this.
_compliance_notes.manufacturer: Reported via DevInfo/Man. Some compliance or enrollment restriction policies allowlist specific manufacturers (Dell, HP, Lenovo, Microsoft Surface). Visible in Intune portal.
_compliance_notes.model: Reported via DevInfo/Mod. Visible in Intune portal. No standard compliance check but used in filters and targeting.
_compliance_notes.firmware_ver: Reported via DevDetail/FwV. Visible in Intune portal. No standard compliance check.
_compliance_notes.hw_version: Reported via Device/DevDetail/HwV. Visible in Intune portal. No standard compliance check.
_compliance_notes.device_type_str: Reported via Device/DevDetail/DevTyp. Use 'PC' for standard desktop/laptop.
_compliance_notes.oem: Reported via Device/DevDetail/OEM. Should match manufacturer.
_compliance_notes.locale: Reported via DevInfo/Lang. Affects locale-based targeting rules. Use en-GB, en-US etc.
_compliance_notes.cname: Subject CN used in the MDM enrollment CSR. Visible in the MDM certificate. 'ConfigMgrEnroll' mimics a standard corporate enrollment.
_compliance_notes.mac_address: Sent in SOAP enrollment AdditionalContext. Visible in Intune portal. All-zeros is an obvious fake -- use a realistic OUI (e.g. Dell: A4-C3-F0-xx-xx-xx).
_compliance_notes.hw_dev_id_enroll: HWDevID sent in SOAP enrollment. 64 hex chars. All-zeros is an obvious fake. Derive from a real device: SHA256 of TPM EKpub or SMBIOS UUID.
_compliance_notes.hw_dev_id_syncml: HWDevID reported via DMClient/HWDevID CSP in SyncML. Should match hw_dev_id_enroll for consistency.
_compliance_notes.bitlocker_status: Reported via Device/Vendor/MSFT/BitLocker/Status/DeviceEncryptionStatus. 2 = encrypted. Self-reported, spoofable. NOTE: the HAS-attested 'Require BitLocker' compliance policy uses HealthAttestation CSP and requires a real TPM -- that cannot be spoofed.
_compliance_notes.encryption_compliance: Reported via DeviceStatus/Compliance/EncryptionCompliance. 1 = compliant. Generic encryption check, self-reported. Simpler than Require BitLocker and fully spoofable.
_compliance_notes._section_security: --- SECURITY POSTURE (DeviceStatus CSP -- all self-reported, all spoofable) ---
_compliance_notes.av_status: DeviceStatus/Antivirus/Status. 0 = on and monitoring (compliant). 1 = off. 2 = not monitoring.
_compliance_notes.av_signature_status: DeviceStatus/Antivirus/SignatureStatus. 0 = up to date (compliant). 1 = out of date.
_compliance_notes.antispyware_sig_status: DeviceStatus/Antispyware/SignatureStatus. 0 = up to date (compliant). 1 = out of date.
_compliance_notes.tpm_version: DeviceStatus/TPM/SpecificationVersion. String e.g. '2.0'. Compliance policies requiring TPM check this. Self-reported -- no cryptographic proof of actual TPM presence.
_compliance_notes.secure_boot_state: DeviceStatus/SecureBootState. 1 = enabled (compliant). Self-reported path only -- NOT the HAS-attested Secure Boot check which requires real TPM PCR values.
_compliance_notes._section_defender: --- MICROSOFT DEFENDER (Defender CSP -- all self-reported, all spoofable) ---
_compliance_notes.defender_version: Defender/Health/DefenderVersion. Version string e.g. '4.18.24090.11'. Some policies enforce a minimum Defender version.
_compliance_notes.defender_sig_out_of_date: Defender/Health/SignatureOutOfDate. false = signatures up to date (compliant). true = out of date.
